2604.17153.title: From Legal Text to Executable Decision Models: Evaluating Structured Representations for Legal Decision Model Generation
2604.17153.paper_summary: Transforming legal text into executable decision logic is a longstanding challenge in legal informatics. With the rise of LLMs, this task has gained renewed interest, but remains challenging due to requiring extensive manual coding and evaluation. We use a unique real-world dataset that pairs production-grade decision models with legal text from the Dutch Environment and Planning Act. These models power the Omgevingsloket government platform, where citizens check permit requirements for environmental activities. We study whether intermediate structured representations can improve LLM-based generation of executable decision models from legal text. We compare four input conditions: raw legal text, text enriched with semantic role labels, text enriched with input and output constraints, and text enriched with both. We evaluate along two dimensions: structural evaluation, through similarity to gold decision models with graph kernels and graphs' descriptive statistics, and outcome evaluation, through functional equivalence by executing models on pre-configured test scenarios. Our findings show that I/O constraints provide the dominant improvement (+37-54% similarity over baseline), while semantic role labels show modest improvements. Outcome evaluation shows that generated models match the gold standard on 51-53% of test scenarios, even though generated models are typically smaller and simpler. We find LLMs eliminate redundant pass-through logic that comprises up to 45-55% of nodes. Importantly, structural similarity and outcome equivalence are complementary: structural similarity does not guarantee outcome equivalence, and vice versa. To facilitate reproducibility, we publicly release our dataset of 95 production decision models with associated legal text and all experimental code.
2604.17153.paper_summary_zh: 將法律文本轉換為可執行的決策邏輯一直是法律資訊學中的一大挑戰。隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）的興起，這項任務重新引起了關注，但由於需要大量的手動編碼和評估，仍然充滿挑戰。我們使用一個獨特的真實世界數據集，將生產級決策模型與荷蘭環境與規劃法的法律文本配對。這些模型為Omgevingsloket政府平台提供支持，公民可以在此查詢環境活動的許可要求。我們研究中間結構化表示是否能改善基於LLM的法律文本可執行決策模型的生成。我們比較了四種輸入條件：原始法律文本、增強了語義角色標籤的文本、增強了輸入和輸出約束的文本，以及同時增強了這兩者的文本。我們從兩個維度進行評估：結構評估，通過與金標準決策模型的相似性使用圖內核和圖的描述性統計來進行；結果評估，通過在預配置的測試場景上執行模型來進行功能等價性評估。我們的研究結果顯示，I/O約束提供了主要的改進（相較基線提高37-54%的相似性），而語義角色標籤則顯示出適度的改善。結果評估顯示，生成的模型在51-53%的測試場景中符合金標準，即使生成的模型通常較小且較簡單。我們發現LLMs消除了多餘的通過邏輯，這部分佔據了多達45-55%的節點。重要的是，結構相似性和結果等價性是互補的：結構相似性並不保證結果等價性，反之亦然。為了促進可重複性，我們公開發布了包含95個生產決策模型及其相關法律文本的數據集和所有實驗代碼。
2604.17153.author: David Graus et.al.
